groups.label: Inspect packets and render review artifacts

notes: `--example-output` prints a minimal proof-plan packet.
The command emits `cautilus.claim_proof_plan.v1`: a source-ref-backed plan, not a verdict that the repo is correct.
Default discovery starts from adapter-owned entries or README.md/AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md and follows repo-local Markdown links to depth 3.
Use `--source` repeatedly when the host repo wants an explicit truth-surface inventory instead of the conservative defaults.

Use `--adapter <adapter.yaml>` to apply an explicit adapter file (for example a proposed-hints adapter) without writing into the scanned repo; useful for read-only corpus measurement and hint dry-runs before maintainer ratification.

If `--output` points to an existing claim packet and neither `--previous` nor `--from-scratch` is given, the existing packet is auto-used as `--previous` so reviewed labels and evidence refs carry forward.
Pass `--from-scratch` to force first-discovery semantics when the existing output should be discarded; this is mutually exclusive with `--previous`.
Use `--previous <claims.json> --refresh-plan` when an agent workflow needs deterministic refresh planning without a separate refresh command.
Refresh-plan packets include `refreshSummary` so agents can explain changed sources, changed claims, and next choices without guessing raw JSON fields.
Refresh-plan output is not a saved claim map update; write it to a `refresh-plan*.json` artifact instead of the configured latest/reviewed/evidenced claim-state paths.
Emits the no-input orientation packet for Cautilus Agent.
The packet reads binary, agent-surface, adapter, and claim-state availability before a branch is selected.
Emits `cautilus.packet_inspection.v1` with `schemaVersion`, `topLevelKeys`, `arrayCounts`, and `selectorHints`.
`arrayCounts` reflects the literal top-level array fields of any JSON object input, even for unknown schemas.

`selectorHints` is populated only for known Cautilus packet schemas (currently `claim_eval_plan` and `claim_review_input`); each hint names a canonical jq path so operators don't have to guess field names like `.plans` versus `.evalPlans`.
Summarizes an existing `cautilus.claim_proof_plan.v1` packet without rescanning.
`--sample-claims` includes a bounded set of stable claim fields for agents that need examples before choosing the next branch.
Includes `evidenceSatisfaction` so satisfied claims and their evidence refs are visible from the status summary.
This command emits `cautilus.claim_status_summary.v1` for agents that need current status before choosing review, test, scenario, or alignment work.
Prepares deterministic `cautilus.claim_review_input.v1` clusters for agent or subagent review.
Rejects stale claim packets by default; staleness means recorded claim sources changed, not merely that generated claim artifacts were committed later.
May include `possibleEvidenceRefs` from adapter `evidence_roots`, but those refs are hints and cannot satisfy claims.
`--action-bucket` focuses review input on one status action bucket and records non-matching claims under `skippedClaims`.
Skips claims already marked `evidenceStatus=satisfied` or already reviewed by default and records them under `skippedClaims` so review budget stays focused on unresolved heuristic claims.
The command does not call an LLM and does not mark claims satisfied.
Applies deterministic label and evidence updates from `cautilus.claim_review_result.v1` to an existing claim packet.
Rejects stale claim packets by default before merging review results; packet-only HEAD drift is reported without blocking this command.
`evidenceStatus=satisfied` requires direct or verified evidence refs; possible evidence refs cannot satisfy a claim.
`--example-output` prints a minimal extraction-input packet.
Emits `cautilus.claim_extraction_input.v1`: sources with content hashes, the embedded product-owned extraction template with version and hash, merged classification hints, and extraction bounds.
The command is deterministic and makes no model calls; the Cautilus Agent consumes the packet to extract claims and answers with `cautilus.claim_extraction_result.v1`.
Scan-scope confirmation does not authorize agent extraction; running the extraction is an LLM activity behind a separate extraction-budget confirmation owned by the agent flow.
Use `--adapter <adapter.yaml>` to apply an explicit adapter file without writing into the scanned repo; useful for read-only corpus measurement before maintainer ratification.
This slice emits `extractionTarget: first-extraction` only; refresh composition over a previous packet is a later slice.
`--example-input` prints a minimal `cautilus.claim_extraction_result.v1` packet an agent would author.

Validates the agent extraction result, anchors every excerpt as a whitespace-normalized substring of the raw source content, and composes a binary-owned `cautilus.claim_proof_plan.v1` packet with `extractionMode: agent`.

Claims with fabricated, missing-primary, empty, over-long, duplicate-fingerprint, or out-of-scope excerpts are rejected into `extractionAudit.rejectedClaims`, never silently dropped; rejections do not fail the command.
A templateHash mismatch, a result that fails schema validation, or drifted sources without `--allow-stale-sources` fail the whole command and write no packet.
`claimFingerprint` is the sha256 of the normalized primary verbatim excerpt, unified with heuristic-mode fingerprints.
Agent packets should target a path other than the adapter's claim `state_path` until the repo decides its refresh-gate policy for agent mode.
Emits `cautilus.claim_eval_plan.v1` for reviewed `cautilus-eval` claims that are ready to verify.
Skips claims that already have `evidenceStatus=satisfied`; they do not need a new eval fixture plan by default.
Rejects stale claim packets by default before planning eval fixtures; generated claim artifact commits do not make the source inventory stale.
The command plans eval fixtures but does not write host-owned fixtures, prompts, runners, or policy.
Emits `cautilus.claim_validation_report.v1` and exits non-zero when packet shape or evidence refs are invalid.
The command validates existing packets but does not mutate claims or search for evidence.
`--example-input` prints a minimal valid conversation-review input packet.
This read-only surface stays scenario-centric: it links normalized chatbot threads to scenario proposals instead of acting like a generic audit browser.
Runs one checked-in evaluation fixture through an adapter-owned runner, then evaluates the observed packet.
The four first-class presets are `dev/repo`, `dev/skill`, `app/chat`, and `app/prompt`.
`--runtime codex|claude|fixture|product` selects the runner backend placeholder; use `fixture` for cheap command-routing smoke tests, and `product` for headless product-path runners.
Evaluates one already-observed packet without launching the host runner again.
The packet schema selects the preset: `dev/repo`, `dev/skill`, `app/chat`, or `app/prompt`.
Reads a selected finalist on an optimizer-untouchable final acceptance set to estimate the generalization gap (see docs/contracts/final-acceptance-set.md).
Excludes and flags any acceptance scenario id that also appears in the search held-out set, and refuses only when no clean scenario remains.
Advisory only: emits `cautilus.acceptance_report.v1` with the gap, a reliability flag, and the held-out exposure count; it never auto-applies or auto-rejects a candidate.
Acceptance results must be produced with `mode: acceptance`; they must never feed evidence input or improve.
Compares a sandboxed temporary skill variant against a baseline run, optional exemplar, rubric phrases, and declared source-coverage obligations.
The command does not install, mutate, or execute skills; host-owned runners provide the preserved baseline and variant outputs.
Emits `cautilus.skill_clone_experiment_report.v1` with `variant_ran`, coverage delta, rubric match, isolation notes, and a promotion recommendation.
Discovers live app targets for app evals.
Runs one bounded live app request and writes `cautilus.live_run_invocation_result.v1`.
The adapter may still delegate actual runtime launch to a consumer-owned command template.
Turns draft scenarios or normalized catalog candidates into `cautilus.live_run_invocation_request_batch.v1`.
Runs an explicit live request batch for app evals and writes `cautilus.live_run_invocation_batch_result.v1`.
Each attempt writes nested request/result artifacts under `<output-file>.d/runs/`.

commands.example: cautilus evaluate live persona --workspace . --simulator-request-file /tmp/simulator-request.json --simulator-result-file /tmp/simulator-result.json --backend fixture --fixture-results-file fixtures/live-run/persona-fixture.json
notes: Turns one persona_prompt simulator request into one canonical simulator result packet for app evals.
The canonical JSON output preserves the full ranked proposal list for agents.
The output packet also emits an `attentionView`, which is a bounded human-facing shortlist derived from the full ranked set.
Default `doctor` validates the unnamed checked-in adapter path.
Use `cautilus doctor --repo-root . --next-action` when you want one current onboarding step plus the exact follow-up loop.
If your repo intentionally keeps only named adapters under `.agents/cautilus-adapters/`, use `cautilus doctor --repo-root . --adapter-name <name>` for repo-scope validation.
A ready repo payload includes `first_bounded_run`, which adds a starter `evaluate fixture -> evaluate observation` packet loop.
`--example-input` prints a minimal valid report-input packet.
`humanReviewFindings` entries require `severity` and `message`, with optional `path`.
If you need a minimal valid report packet before running review, start with `cautilus evaluate report build --example-input`.
That example shows the minimum `humanReviewFindings` shape: `{ "severity": "concern", "message": "...", "path": "optional" }`.

commands.example: cautilus evaluate review feedback build --source-kind hitl --source-ref docs/internal/handoff.md --method-family claim_discovery --method-id linked_markdown_scan --source-scope docs/specs/index.spec.md --proposal-source-ref docs/specs/index.spec.md:14 --disposition reframed --review-note "Useful signal, but the claim name needed narrowing." --output /tmp/cautilus-review/review-feedback.json
notes: Emits `cautilus.review_feedback.v1` so Cautilus Agent can normalize source-bound review outcomes without becoming the review authority.
The disposition vocabulary is about review usefulness, not test pass/fail status.
Emits `cautilus.review_feedback_summary.v1` for explicitly selected `cautilus.review_feedback.v1` packets.
Counts review-useful dispositions by method family without discovering active-run defaults or treating dispositions as pass/fail status.
Use `--prompt-file` plus `--schema-file` for ad-hoc bounded review without first building a review packet.
Omit those flags when the adapter or review prompt input provides the default prompt and schema.
`improve` is the improvement front door; use it only after the claim and eval proof surface are explicit.
This command prepares a bounded improvement packet from report, review, and history evidence.
Prepares a bounded search packet above an explicit improve input and held-out evidence.
Runs the bounded search and emits a checkpointed result packet; it is not an open-ended retry loop.
Produces a bounded improvement proposal from an explicit improve input.
Builds the reviewable revision artifact from an accepted improvement proposal.
